summary: Admissions to city juvenile detention centers rose sharply through Fiscal 2025, which ACS attributed to an increase in arrests, including those tied to gun violence and other public-safety concerns. That trend reversed in the first months of Fiscal 2026 — the first year-over-year admissions decline since Fiscal 2020, according to ACS's own report — though the detention population kept climbing anyway because youth are staying much longer once admitted.

sources.label: ACS, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report
